skill.name: youtube-influencer-finder-api-skill
skill.description: Find YouTube influencer profiles through the BrowserAct API. The skill runs a Python helper that submits search keywords and upload-date filters, then returns channel statistics, social links, and available contact details.
skill.summary: Find YouTube creators with channel stats, social links, and available contact details.
skill.author: browser-act

security_audit.summary: Static findings for network access and environment-variable access are true positives, but they support the advertised BrowserAct API workflow rather than showing malicious exfiltration. The external-command and weak-cryptography findings are false positives from Markdown examples and ordinary words, while the combined network, token, and script execution behavior still warrants a medium-risk warning.

medium_findings.title: Third-party API Receives User Queries and Bearer Token
medium_findings.description: The helper reads BROWSERACT_API_KEY and sends it as an Authorization bearer token to the BrowserAct workflow API. This is expected for the skill, but users should understand that their search terms and token are sent to an external service.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code directly constructs a bearer Authorization header from the environment variable and uses it on BrowserAct API requests. This confirms network and credential access, although it matches the documented purpose.

medium_findings.title: Automated Influencer Contact Data Collection
medium_findings.description: The skill is designed to return social links and available contact email data for YouTube creators. This can be legitimate marketing research, but it may raise privacy, consent, and platform-policy concerns depending on use.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documented output fields include creator contact email and social links. The risk is contextual rather than malicious, so the confidence is high for capability and moderate for harmful use.

low_findings.title: Documented Python Script Execution
low_findings.description: The Markdown instructs the agent to run a local Python helper with keyword and upload-date arguments. The script uses argv values as API payload fields, so I did not find evidence of shell interpolation inside the Python code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The static external-command hits are mostly Markdown backticks, while the actual command runs a fixed Python script. The remaining risk is operational because agents may execute the helper on behalf of users.

low_findings.title: False Positive Weak Cryptography and Reconnaissance Hits
low_findings.description: The weak-cryptography and system-reconnaissance detections appear to come from prose such as output field names, API-key guidance, and marketing claims. I did not find evidence of cryptographic code or host reconnaissance behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Reviewed lines show documentation text and a task identifier assignment, not cryptographic primitives or system inventory commands. Confidence is limited because this finding groups several static patterns.

dangerous_patterns.title: Network and Credential Access Combination
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill combines a local script, environment-variable credential access, and outbound network requests. I did not find confirmed malicious intent, but this pattern should remain visible to users during installation.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The individual elements are directly present in the skill and script. The pattern is risky but appears aligned with the stated API integration rather than covert behavior.

content.value_statement: Finding qualified YouTube creators takes time because channel stats, contact links, and recent activity are scattered. This skill uses the BrowserAct API to collect structured creator profiles from keyword and upload-date filters.
actual_capabilities: Runs a Python helper for the BrowserAct YouTube Influencer Finder workflow.
Accepts search keywords and an upload-date filter.
Polls the BrowserAct task status until completion or timeout.
Returns channel statistics such as views, videos, subscribers, and registration date.
Returns visible social links and available contact email fields from the workflow output.
Reports missing or invalid BrowserAct API key errors to the user.
limitations: Requires a valid BROWSERACT_API_KEY environment variable.
Sends search terms and workflow requests to the BrowserAct API.
Can only return data exposed by the BrowserAct workflow response.
May take several minutes because it polls the external workflow service.

use_cases.title: Campaign Creator Shortlists
use_cases.description: Build a targeted list of YouTube creators for a product launch using niche keywords and recent upload filters.

use_cases.title: Market Research Profiles
use_cases.description: Compare channel size, publishing activity, countries, and public links across creators in a research category.

use_cases.title: Outreach Preparation
use_cases.description: Collect available creator contact fields and social links before starting a compliant outreach workflow.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Keyword Search
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner search for creators in one niche.
prompt_templates.prompt: Find YouTube influencers for the keyword "sustainable fashion" with uploads from This Month. Summarize the most relevant profiles.

prompt_templates.title: Recent Creator Discovery
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for YouTube creators about "home fitness" who uploaded This Week. Highlight subscriber counts, channel links, and available social links.

prompt_templates.title: Campaign Fit Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Evaluate a shortlist for a marketing campaign.
prompt_templates.prompt: Find YouTube influencers for "budget travel" using This Month. Group results by audience size and note any available contact fields.

prompt_templates.title: Competitive Research Sweep
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced research across a competitor niche.
prompt_templates.prompt: Research YouTube creators for "AI productivity tools" with uploads from This Year. Compare channel statistics, countries, descriptions, and outreach readiness.

output_examples.input: Keyword: tech reviewer. Upload date: This Month.
output_examples.output: A ranked creator profile summary with channel names, subscriber counts, total views, recent activity context, channel links, and available public contact fields.

output_examples.input: Keyword: vegan recipes. Upload date: This Week.
output: Creator shortlist with active channels first.
Visible social links grouped with each channel profile.
Notes about missing contact fields or incomplete channel data.

output_examples.input: Keyword: gaming accessories. Upload date: This Year.
output_examples.output: A market research summary comparing audience size, channel descriptions, countries, upload recency, and partnership outreach signals.

best_practices: Use specific niche keywords to reduce irrelevant creator matches.
Confirm outreach compliance before using contact details from results.
Store BrowserAct API keys in environment variables, not in prompts or files.
anti_patterns: Do not use broad keywords when you need a focused campaign shortlist.
Do not treat returned contact data as permission to send unsolicited bulk messages.
Do not retry repeatedly after an invalid authorization error.

faq.question: What API key does this skill require?
faq.answer: It requires a BrowserAct API key set as BROWSERACT_API_KEY in the environment.

faq.question: Does this skill scrape YouTube directly from my machine?
faq.answer: The local script calls the BrowserAct workflow API, which performs the configured workflow externally.

faq.question: Can it find creator email addresses?
faq.answer: It can return available contact email fields when the BrowserAct workflow includes them in the result.

faq.question: Which upload-date filters are supported?
faq.answer: The documented filters are Today, This Week, This Month, and This Year.

faq.question: How long does a search take?
faq.answer: The script may run for several minutes because it polls BrowserAct until the task finishes or times out.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: It is publishable with a warning because it uses network access, an API key, and third-party data collection.
